message:
[LOW] fcp-streaming/reconnect: clamp delay_for_attempt to non-negative finite

Duration::from_secs_f64 panics on negative, NaN, or infinite input. A
misconfigured backoff_multiplier (negative literal, NaN, or large
enough to overflow to +inf at high attempt counts) could turn a
recoverable reconnect into a process abort.

Clamp the jittered delay to [0, max_delay] in finite f64 space before
constructing the Duration. Two regression tests cover negative-multiplier
and NaN-multiplier paths.

Found during fcp-streaming audit (post-2ede04cd reconnect-storm fix).
The reconnect-storm scenario itself is properly addressed by 2ede04cd:
backoff is reset only after a real message arrives, not after TCP
handshake — so a peer that opens-and-immediately-closes no longer
triggers a zero-backoff loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
